name: stride-security-review
description: AI-powered security review for code changes via the /stride-security-review:security-review slash command. Two scan modes (diff / --full), seven framework rule packs (Android/Kotlin, Django/Python, Express/Node.js, iOS/Swift, Phoenix/Elixir, Rails/Ruby, React/Next.js) covering the full mobile + web + Node ecosystem with semantic-analysis vulnerability detection. Multi-platform CI/CD rule pack (8 platforms), Web defense-in-depth pack covering security headers (CSP/HSTS/X-Frame-Options/cookie flags), MAESTRO 7-layer agentic-AI classification with explicit non-AI mapping, RCI critique loop, baseline suppression, surgical-fix patches, SARIF v2.1.0 output for GitHub Code Scanning, --fail-on severity gating for CI, --base <ref> for PR-against-base diffs, and --considerations <source> (considerations mode) which verifies a task's security_considerations were actually mitigated by the diff via a per-consideration mitigated/partial/unmitigated verdict. Ships a reference GitHub Actions workflow and a TAP 13 eval runner over 72 fixtures with positive-and-negative-control coverage (including considerations-mode controls), plus golden-file tests for the deterministic SARIF/dedup/fingerprint/fail-on transforms. Loosely based on anthropics/claude-code-security-review.
